title: Microsoft Just Shocked The Entire AI World: 7 New AI Models
summary: Microsoft released seven in-house AI models including MAI-Thinking-1, Scout, Codename MDASH, and Majorana 2 as part of a full-stack strategy to compete with OpenAI. The moves include a reasoning model, an always-on agent, and advances in quantum hardware, positioning Microsoft as both OpenAI's partner and competitor.
writeup: At Build 2026, Microsoft unveiled a seven-model family trained in-house, headlined by MAI Thinking One, a 35B-parameter reasoning model built on commercially-licensed data without distillation from third-party systems. The release signals a deliberate shift from renting frontier intelligence through OpenAI and Anthropic toward owning the full model stack inside Azure, controlling costs and margins end to end. Alongside the models, Microsoft launched the Microsoft IQ intelligence layer (Work IQ, Fabric IQ, Foundry IQ, Web IQ), the Scout always-on autopilot agent with governed Entra identity, a 100-agent security system codenamed Mdash, and Majorana 2, a topological quantum chip 1,000 times more reliable than its predecessor.
key_points: MAI Thinking One is Microsoft's first reasoning model trained from scratch on clean commercially-licensed data, with 35 billion active parameters and a context window reported variously as 128,000 or 256,000 tokens, already available in private preview on Microsoft AI Foundry.

Microsoft claims that after fine-tuning for McKinsey, its own models outperformed OpenAI GPT 4.5 on quality while projecting roughly 10 times better cost efficiency based on public pricing data scaled across model sizes.

Microsoft IQ, now generally available, is the unified intelligence layer connecting Copilot and agents to organizational context: Work IQ covers people, emails, documents, and meetings inside Microsoft 365 (APIs opening June 16); Fabric IQ provides a semantic ontology for structured business data; Foundry IQ handles unstructured documents and policies; and Web IQ gives agents real-world grounding via MCP-native search, returning relevant blocks nearly 2.5 times faster than the next best alternative.

Scout, Microsoft's first autopilot agent, operates under its own governed Entra identity with task-scoped credentials, integrates across Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint, and handles coordination work such as cross-timezone scheduling, deliverable tracking, and risk flagging, learning over time through Work IQ.

The Mdash security system deploys more than 100 specialized agents to reason about data flows, business logic, and exploit chains in code, targeting vulnerability classes a conventional static scanner would miss.

Majorana 2, Microsoft's next-generation topological quantum chip developed with help from its agentic AI research system Microsoft Discoveries, achieves a mean qubit lifetime of 20 seconds with some instances lasting up to 1 minute, representing a 1,000-fold reliability improvement over the previous generation.
